commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: Being “wise as to what is good” means doing good works, while being “guileless as to what is evil” means avoiding unrighteous deeds. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: The apostle admits that he rejoices because of the hearts of the innocent, and [in this verse] he gives a kind of definition of being as a child.

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: On account of the hearts of the innocent, the apostle, in the Epistle to the Romans, owns that he rejoices, and furnishes a kind of definition of children, so to speak, when he says, "I would have you wise toward good, but simple towards evil."

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
